title: Rewrite README 'The five layers' as a fully-labelled corpus-topology table with verified authority paths
rationale: The current 'five layers' section (README lines 43-60) is the corpus's container map — which repo owns which layer and what the authority rule is — but it carries ZERO of the four mandatory labels (tier/status/pathway/trace), so it fails the hard constraint (worth zero at any score, T5/GR-3) despite being only 179 words. My lens walked the topology and confirmed every layer→repo→authority binding on disk (ls, 2026-07-10): canon=agentprivacy-docs (papers/v6 + register), substrate=spellweb (nodes.ts/edges.ts/graph-coherence-audit.mjs), structure=cityofmages grimoire + game42 (a STANDALONE repo with its own head game-of-42.json, which the current comma-joined 'cityofmages, game42' obscures), surface=agentprivacy_master (public/tomes/workshops carry vertex+resident_mage), harness=this repo + tig_zk_loop. The rewrite (a) labels every claim so the section can pass the hard constraint, (b) traces the 'mirrors cite, never edit' rule to the mirror's ACTUAL header wording ('generated, never hand-edited; the register wins') instead of a paraphrase, and (c) surfaces the live register_head C96/next-free-C97, fixing the E-5 staleness the current README propagates by omission. Every drawable container fact is preserved; each now defends at a tier it can actually hold (path-existence=PROVEN, canon rules=REPORTED, audit exit=0=REPORTED via frontier).
hardConstraintNote: Every claim carries all four labels. The five table rows share one label line: PROVEN (each path re-lists via ls, trace = the path named) · IMPLEMENTED (real dirs/files) · connect (topology coordinating repos). The substrate audit exit-0 is split out as REPORTED · trace frontier.json corpusCensus (a number, so cited to frontier per GR-1, not re-run). The resolution rule is REPORTED · SPEC · protect · trace mirror authority_note + ERRATA E-5. The mirror 'cite, never edit' + C96/C97 head is PROVEN (header read 2026-07-10) · IMPLEMENTED · protect · trace the mirror path's authority_note/register_head + ERRATA E-5. No claim lacks a resolving path (GR-9). This is a method section, so it contains NO MYTH (ADMISSION §3): any unsourced 'beautiful sentence' would make a pathway a linear combination of the others, det(Σ)→0. Guide/soulbis were dropped from the surface row rather than cited untraced — my readable corpus grants only agentprivacy_master as surface.
killedLeverCitations: K-1 wing-based seat assignment — not re-proposed; this lever concerns corpus-container topology, not seat assignment
K-3 mana-as-attestation — respected; no claim in this section treats 🪢 VRC mana as proof

K-4 stale blades sheet — honoured, not re-proposed: I trace the register head to the LIVE mirror header (C96/C97) rather than the stale docs README (C89), which is exactly K-4's lesson to read live authority not stale prose
mapText:
## The five layers

Five corpora, not one; a claim true at one layer is false at another. Each
path below was confirmed on disk (`ls`, 2026-07-10).

| layer | holds | repo → authority path |
|---|---|---|
| **canon** | model, maths, C-ids | `agentprivacy-docs` → `papers/v6/`; `research/CONJECTURE_REGISTER_V6.md` |
| **substrate** | the shared knowledge graph | `spellweb` → `src/data/nodes.ts`, `edges.ts`; gate `scripts/graph-coherence-audit.mjs` |
| **structure** | vertices, workshops, tomes, cast, mana | `cityofmages` → `grimoire/city_of_mages_grimoire_v1_9_1.json`; `game42` → `game-of-42.json` |
| **surface** | rendered sites, ceremonies, mirrors | `agentprivacy_master` → `public/tomes/workshops/*.md` (`vertex` + `resident_mage`) |
| **harness** | the result loops | this repo + `tig_zk_loop` → `engine/conform.mjs` + the gate ladder |

All rows: **PROVEN · IMPLEMENTED · connect**, trace = the path named. Substrate's
clean audit (exit 0) is **REPORTED · trace `frontier.json` corpusCensus**.

**Resolution rule** — **REPORTED · SPEC · protect** · trace mirror
`authority_note` + `ERRATA.md` E-5: when prose and register disagree, the
register wins and the prose takes an erratum; cross-canon conflicts escalate,
never resolve locally.

**Mirrors cite, never edit** — **PROVEN · IMPLEMENTED · protect** · trace
`agentprivacy_master/src/data/conjecture-register-v6-mirror.json`
`authority_note`/`register_head`, read 2026-07-10: the mirror is generated
from the register, never hand-edited; its `register_head` reads **C96, next
free C97** — the docs root README's C89 is stale prose, not an authority.
